skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations. Triggers: flux, image generation, ai image, text to image, stable diffusion, generate image, ai art, midjourney alternative, dall-e alternative, text2img, t2i, image generator, ai picture, create image with ai, generative ai, ai illustration, grok image, gemini image
skill.summary: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: inference-sh-8

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 47 patterns in SKILL.md documentation file. All findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the file is markdown documentation containing CLI usage examples, not executable code. Backtick patterns are markdown code block formatting. URLs are documentation links. The skill requires restricted Bash(infsh *) access for the inference.sh CLI, which is a legitimate external command pattern with no command injection risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Documentation Patterns Detected as Code
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected shell command patterns in markdown documentation. SKILL.md is a documentation file containing CLI usage examples in markdown code blocks. The backtick characters are markdown formatting syntax, not executable Ruby/shell code. No actual code execution occurs from this file.

content.value_statement: Create professional AI images without learning multiple platforms. Access FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, and Reve through one unified CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using FLUX Dev, FLUX.2 Klein, and FLUX.2 models
Access Google Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash for image generation
Use xAI Grok Imagine for multi-aspect image creation
Create 2K-4K cinematic quality images with Seedream 4.5 and 4.0
Generate images with accurate text rendering using Reve
Upscale images professionally with Topaz upscaler
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account
Image generation depends on external inference.sh service availability
Free tier may have usage limits and credit requirements
Cannot run models locally - all generation happens on inference.sh servers

use_cases.title: Marketing and Social Media Content
use_cases.description: Create eye-catching visuals for social media posts, ads, and marketing campaigns without hiring a designer.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and social media managers

use_cases.title: Product Mockups and Prototyping
use_cases.description: Generate product visualization concepts and mockups for client presentations and design reviews.

use_cases.title: Concept Art and Storyboarding
use_cases.description: Rapidly visualize characters, environments, and scenes for games, films, and creative projects.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a cat astronaut floating in space with stars in the background

prompt_templates.title: Product Photography Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional product photo of a coffee mug on a wooden table with studio lighting and shallow depth of field

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Landscape
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cinematic landscape with mountains and a lake at golden hour, photorealistic style, 4K quality, dramatic lighting

prompt_templates.title: Text-in-Image Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a poster design that displays the text HELLO WORLD in bold modern typography with a minimalist background
prompt_templates.scenario: Text rendering using Reve model for accurate typography

output_examples.input: Professional product photo of a coffee mug, studio lighting
output_examples.output: High-quality product image showing a coffee mug with professional studio lighting, clean background, suitable for e-commerce listings

output_examples.input: Cyberpunk city at night, neon lights, rain-slicked streets
output_examples.output: Atmospheric cyberpunk cityscape with glowing neon signs, reflective wet streets, and futuristic architecture in 16:9 aspect ratio

best_practices: Write detailed prompts including subject, style, lighting, and composition for best results
Choose the appropriate model for your use case: FLUX for versatility, Seedream for quality, Reve for text
Start with lower-resolution models for testing, then upscale final selections with Topaz
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like make it look good without specifying what good means for your use case
Do not use the service for generating copyrighted or trademarked content without proper authorization
Avoid expecting photorealistic human faces for commercial use due to potential uncanny valley effects

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh in your terminal, then run infsh login to authenticate with your account.

faq.question: Which model should I use for my project?
faq.answer: Use FLUX Dev for general purpose generation, Seedream 4.5 for highest quality 4K images, Reve for images with text, and Grok Imagine for multiple aspect variations.

faq.question: Can I use generated images commercially?
faq.answer: Check the inference.sh terms of service and the specific model license. Most models allow commercial use but restrictions may apply.

faq.question: How do I upscale low-resolution images?
faq.answer: Use the Topaz upscaler model by running infsh app run falai/topaz-image-upscaler with your image URL as input.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The service typically outputs PNG or JPEG formats. Input images for editing or upscaling should be in common web formats like JPG or PNG.

faq.question: How much does image generation cost?
faq.answer: Pricing varies by model and resolution. Check the inference.sh pricing page for current rates. Some models offer free tier usage with limits.
